Synthetic Canadians: Comparing Synthetic Survey Data Generators

2019 Democracy Checkup — Political Attitudes Validation Study

Author

Synthetic Opinion Lab

Published

March 14, 2026

Overview

This report validates three synthetic data generation approaches against the 2019 Democracy Checkup (DC19), a national survey of Canadian political attitudes (n = 2,747).

The 19 variables span five substantive domains: government satisfaction, institutional confidence (4 institutions), political efficacy, immigration attitudes, social conservatism, and economic positions, plus party identification and vote intention.

Three generators are compared:

Generator N synthetic Overall quality score
LLM (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) 100 0.743
IRT (Graded Response Model) 1,000 0.530
Agent (Opinion dynamics) 500 0.501

Correlation Structure

Frobenius distance from real correlation matrix:
  IRT                 : Frobenius = 3.88, r(lower tri) = 0.203
  LLM                 : Frobenius = 4.94, r(lower tri) = 0.610
  Agent               : Frobenius = 12.95, r(lower tri) = 0.084

Regression Replication: Vote Intention Predictors

Summary

Dimension Real IRT LLM Agent
N respondents 2,747 1,000 100 500
Overall quality score — 0.530 0.743 0.501
Valid response range ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅
Mean-level accuracy — Partial Good Partial
Variance accuracy — Good Moderate Moderate
Correlation structure — Moderate Best Weak

LLM achieves the highest fidelity (0.743). IRT (0.530) produces good variance. Agent (0.501) converges to moderate uniform intensity.
